title: monticalia pulchella subsp. pulchella
intro: Monticalia pulchella subsp. pulchella, also known as the 'Yellow-flowering Monticalia', is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use in various forms, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are said to promote relief from coughs, colds, and other respiratory problems. Additionally, the plant's leaves and stems can be used to make poultices, which are applied topically to treat skin irritations and wounds.
